title: Lesson Quiz: Short-Term and Long-Term Planning
description: Assessment of the concept that Academic and career timelines.

choices.text: A goal or step that can be completed in the near future
choices.feedback: Correct. A short-term plan focuses on actions or goals that can happen soon.

choices.text: A goal that takes many years with no smaller steps
choices.feedback: That description fits a longer-term direction more than a short-term plan.

choices.text: A reward for finishing a class
choices.feedback: A reward is not the same as a short-term plan.
A school rule is not a short-term plan.

questions.prompt: A timeline can help students organize steps in the order they need to happen.

choices.feedback: A timeline is useful because it helps organize the order of steps.
Correct. Timelines help students see what should happen first, next, and later.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows a long-term goal?

choices.feedback: That is a short-term task, not a long-term goal.

choices.text: Talk to a teacher after class today
choices.feedback: That is a short-term action step.

choices.text: Prepare over time for a future career direction
choices.feedback: Correct. A long-term goal usually takes more time and supports a larger future result.

questions.prompt: Why is it useful to connect short-term steps to a long-term goal?

choices.text: Because it helps students see how current actions support future plans
choices.feedback: This is close, but the strongest answer here is the one that more directly emphasizes organized progress.

choices.text: Because it removes the need to plan ahead
choices.feedback: Connecting steps to a goal strengthens planning; it does not remove the need for it.

choices.text: Because it guarantees every plan will be easy
choices.feedback: Planning can help, but it does not guarantee ease.

choices.text: Because it helps students organize progress toward a larger goal
choices.feedback: Correct. Short-term steps help students move in an organized way toward a bigger goal.

questions.prompt: A long-term goal does not need any short-term steps.

choices.feedback: This is incorrect. Long-term goals are usually stronger when supported by short-term steps.
Correct. Long-term goals usually need smaller steps to support them.
